content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting at the Beach
content.explanation: Let's count the objects we see! You can count them one by one or in groups. 🌊🐚

practice_questions.q: How did you count them?
practice_questions.a: I counted them one by one.

practice_questions.q: Did you see any animals at the seashore?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fill in the Blanks
content.explanation: Look at the picture and fill in the blanks with the correct numbers! ✏️

practice_questions.q: There are ___________ necklaces of shells.
There are ___________ groups of balloons.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Surbhi's Seashell Adventure
content.explanation: Surbhi collected seashells to make beautiful bracelets and necklaces! 🌊💍

content.subject: maths
content.title: Help Manoj with Chikoos
content.explanation: Manoj is selling chikoos. Let's help him count how many trays he needs! 🍐

practice_questions.q: How many trays for 43 chikoos?
How many trays for 35 chikoos?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting with Ten Strips
content.explanation: A ten strip has 10 units. Let's count how many units are in different strips! 📏

practice_questions.q: How many units in a ten strip?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Seashells Tongue Twister
content.explanation: Try saying this tongue twister! It's fun and tricky! 🗣️
content.text: She sells seashells at the seashore.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Let Us Make 100!
content.explanation: Let's see how we can combine different strips to make 100 units! 🎉

practice_questions.q: How many yellow and red units make 100?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Rolling Two Dice
content.explanation: When you roll two dice, you add the numbers together to take that many units from the bank. If you get 10 or more, you can exchange them for a ten strip! 🎲💰
examples: If you roll a 4 and a 5, you take 9 units.
If you roll a 6 and a 5, you take 11 units and exchange for a ten strip.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting through Cards
content.concept: Using cards to represent groups of ten and one.
content.explanation: You can use cards to help count! Each card represents a group of ten shells and one card represents one shell. 📦🐚

content.subject: maths
content.title: Complete the Following
content.explanation: Fill in the blanks with the correct number of tens and ones! 📝

practice_questions.q: So, there are total of 31 shells. Fill in the blanks: ___ tens ___ ones
What is 390 in tens and ones?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Make Numbers with Cards
content.concept: Forming numbers using tens and ones cards.
content.explanation: You can make numbers by combining tens and ones cards! For example, to make 52, use a 50 card and a 2 card. 🎴✨
examples: To make 27, use a 20 card and a 7 card.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Which is More?
content.explanation: Let's compare two groups of oranges! Which group has more? 🍊🍊

practice_questions.q: Which is more in number: 43 oranges or 34 oranges?
practice_questions.a: 43 oranges are more because they have 4 tens.

practice_questions.q: Which is less: 27 oranges or 72 oranges?

content.subject: maths
content.title: More or Less
content.explanation: Fill in the blanks with 'more' or 'less' to compare the numbers! 🆚

practice_questions.q: 67 chikoos are _______ than 76 chikoos.
53 shells are _______ than 35 shells.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Make Two-Digit Numbers
content.concept: Creating two-digit numbers with flash cards.
content.explanation: Use flash cards to create two-digit numbers! Can you make a number greater than 50? 🎴🔢

practice_questions.q: Which is the smallest two-digit number you can make?
Which is the largest two-digit number you can make?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Finding the Largest Number
content.concept: Identifying the largest number using the same digits.
content.explanation: Let's play with numbers! You can rearrange the digits to find the largest number possible. For example, with the digits 3, 5, and 7, the largest number you can make is 753! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What is the largest number you can make with the digits 6, 2, and 1?
What is the largest number you can make with the digits 8, 0, and 3?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Make Your Own Questions
content.explanation: Now it's your turn! Can you think of your own questions about two-digit numbers? Try to describe them like in the examples! 🤔

content.subject: maths
content.title: Decreasing Order Challenge
content.explanation: Can you start from the largest number and go to the smallest? Make sure to pass through each number at least once! 🚀

content.subject: maths
content.title: The Snake Boat Race
content.explanation: In Kerala, there's a fun race called Vallam Kali! Let's see which boat is in the lead and where others are positioned. 🐍🚣‍♂️

practice_questions.q: Which colour boat came first in the race?
Which colour boat is likely to come third?
At which positions are blue and green boats?
Write the position of pink and orange boats.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Reading the Calendar
content.concept: Understanding dates and events on a calendar.
content.explanation: Let's look at the calendar for August! Can you find out when Onam and Independence Day are celebrated? 📅🎉

practice_questions.q: Onam will be celebrated on which day of the month?
When is Independence Day celebrated?
When is Raksha Bandhan in August?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Identifying Shapes of Musical Instruments
content.concept: Recognizing shapes of different musical instruments.
content.explanation: Look at the musical instruments! Can you find which ones are shaped like a drum or a matka? Let's explore shapes together! 🎶🔍

practice_questions.q: Circle the musical instruments that are similar in shape to a drum.
Tick the musical instruments that are similar in shape to a matka.
How is the shape of harmonium different from the shape of a drum?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Faces, Edges, and Corners
content.concept: Identifying faces, edges, and corners of solid objects.
content.explanation: Let's explore solid shapes! A cube has 6 faces, 12 edges, and 8 corners. Can you find objects around you that have these features? 🧊📦

practice_questions.q: How many stars do we need for a cube?
How many edges does a cuboid have?

content.subject: maths
content.title: How Many Items Do We Need?
content.concept: Calculating the number of decorative items for solid shapes.
content.explanation: Let's decorate shapes! If a cube has 6 faces, how many stars do we need? Let's count together! 🌟✨
examples: Cube: 6 stars, 12 ribbon stripes, 8 pompom balls

practice_questions.q: For a cuboid, how many stars do we need?
For a cone, how many edges does it have?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Guess the Object
content.concept: Identifying objects based on their features.
content.explanation: Blindfold your friend and let them guess the object you describe! Can they find something with 3 corners? 🤔👀

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fun with Ginladi
content.explanation: Let's count the beads on the ginladi! How many beads do you see? Let's write the numbers together! 🎉🔢

practice_questions.q: How many beads are there in ginladi?
What number comes after 44?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Number Guessing Game
content.concept: Playing a guessing game with numbers.
content.explanation: Think of a number and let your friends guess it! They can ask yes/no questions. Can they guess your number in 10 questions? 🤔🎲

content.subject: maths
content.title: Ants on a Number Line
content.concept: Identifying positions on a number line.
content.explanation: Draw ants on the number line! Where do they sit? Let's find out together! 🐜📏

content.subject: maths
content.title: The Jumping Frog
content.explanation: Look at how Jumpy the frog jumps! Can you see the pattern? 🐸✨

content.subject: maths
content.title: Creating a Number Line
content.concept: Understanding numbers through a number line.
content.explanation: Let's make a number line on the ground! 🌍 You can jump on it and explore numbers. If you jump in steps of two, which numbers will you land on? 🤔

content.subject: maths
content.title: Completing Patterns
content.explanation: Can you fill in the missing numbers in these patterns? Let's see how good you are at spotting patterns! 🕵️‍♂️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Skip Counting Practice
content.concept: Learning skip counting by twos and fives.
content.explanation: Let's skip count! 🎉 Draw on the number chart while skip counting by twos and fives. Can you find the common numbers? 🤩

content.subject: maths
content.title: Jump and Find the Answers
content.concept: Using jumping to solve counting questions.
content.explanation: Jump on your number line and answer these questions! 🤸‍♂️ Will you land on the numbers mentioned? Let's find out!
examples: If you start from 10 and jump in tens, will you land on 100?

practice_questions.q: If you start from 5 and count in fives, will you jump on number 40?
If you start from 4 and count in twos, will you jump on number 17?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Let Us Jump Backward
content.explanation: Let's count backward! 🔙 What comes just before these numbers? Can you fill in the blanks? 📝

content.subject: maths
content.title: Discuss Counting Questions
content.explanation: Let's discuss these counting scenarios! 🤔 Why will Rizwan not say 19 if he starts from 20? Let's think together! 💭

practice_questions.q: Will Rizwan say 19 if he starts counting from 20?
Will Chavi say 43 if she counts in twos from 10?
practice_questions.a: No, because 43 is not in her count.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Exploring Number Patterns
content.explanation: Let's look at the number chart! Can you find what comes just before 10, 20, 30, and 40? 🌟 Numbers follow patterns, and we can discover them together!

practice_questions.q: What comes just before 10?
What comes just before 20?
What comes just before 30?
What comes just before 40?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fill in the Missing Numbers
content.explanation: Look at the green numbers in the number chart: 1, 12, 23, ____, ____, ____, ____, ____, ____, ____. Can you fill in the blanks? 📝

content.subject: maths
content.title: Number Window Activity
content.concept: Finding numbers around a central number.
content.explanation: If the center of your number window is on 28, can you tell me which numbers are above, below, to the right, and to the left? 🔍

practice_questions.q: What number is above 28?
What number is below 28?
What number is on the right of 28?
What number is on the left of 28?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Let us Play — Bingo!
content.concept: Playing Bingo to reinforce number recognition.
content.explanation: Let's play Bingo! 🎉 Fill a 3 × 4 grid with numbers from 2 to 12. When a child rolls two dice, they will call out the sum, and you cross it off your grid. The first to cross all wins!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Nakul and the Shadow Play
content.explanation: Nakul and his friends saw a shadow play called Togalu Gombeyaata at a village fair. They were amazed and wanted to create their own shadows! 🌟 Can you make shadows with your fingers too?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fun with Shadows
content.explanation: Put a torchlight on objects around you and see how their shadows change! 🌞 When is your shadow longest? When is it shortest? Let's find out!

practice_questions.q: Will the size of shadows change if you move the torch?
When is your shadow longest?
When does a shadow vanish?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Let us Trace
content.concept: Tracing and drawing shapes of objects.
content.explanation: Trace the shapes of objects around you like an eraser or a leaf. ✏️ Then, draw the shapes you see! What fun shapes can you create?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Hide and Seek with Shapes
content.concept: Understanding shapes through a fun game.
content.explanation: Circle and Rectangle are playing hide and seek! Can you help them find good hiding spots? 🌟
examples: Circle can hide behind a clock 🕰️
Rectangle can hide in a shoe box 📦

practice_questions.q: Where can Circle hide?
practice_questions.a: Circle can hide in the clock, ball, or behind a book.

practice_questions.q: Where can Rectangle hide?
practice_questions.a: Rectangle can hide in the shoe box, photo frame, or under the table.

practice_questions.q: Where can Triangle and Square hide?
practice_questions.a: Triangle can hide in a corner, and Square can hide behind a chair.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Shapes Join the Game
content.explanation: Triangle and Square join Circle and Rectangle in a fun game of hide and seek! Can you guess where they hide? 🎉

content.subject: maths
content.title: Colour the Carpet
content.explanation: Let's color the shapes! 🎨 Can you color the triangles red, circles green, rectangles yellow, and squares blue?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Catch the Corner Game
content.concept: Understanding corners and edges through a game.
content.explanation: In this game, you need to stand near the corners! Can you name the children near the corners? 🏃‍♂️🏃‍♀️

practice_questions.q: Name the children standing near the corners of the table.
practice_questions.a: Udit, Paramjeet, Wasim, Naysa, Seema, Naveen.

practice_questions.q: Can this game be played around a round table? Why or why not?
practice_questions.a: No, because round tables don't have corners!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Explore Edges and Corners
content.concept: Identifying edges and corners in everyday objects.
content.explanation: Look around you! Can you find objects with edges and corners? 🏠📏

content.subject: maths
content.title: Make a Paper Dog
content.explanation: Let's fold a paper to make a cute dog! 🐶 How many corners does it have?

practice_questions.q: How many corners do objects with curved edges have?
Can you fold the square sheet to get a shape with 4 sides?
practice_questions.a: Yes, it will still have 4 corners!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Create Your Own Patterns
content.explanation: Use bottle caps, erasers, and other objects to create fun patterns! 🌈

content.subject: maths
content.title: Make Leaf Patterns
content.explanation: Collect leaves and paint them to make beautiful patterns! 🍃

content.subject: maths
content.title: Wall Hanging with Shapes
content.concept: Creating a wall hanging using different shapes.
content.explanation: Get creative and make a wall hanging with cutouts of shapes! 🖼️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Extending Patterns
content.concept: Learn how to extend patterns using letters.
content.explanation: Patterns are sequences that repeat. For example, if we have AB, BC, CD, we can continue the pattern! Let's see how it works! 🌈

content.subject: maths
content.title: Extending Number Patterns
content.explanation: Let's help Kikku's mother by extending the number pattern! If we start with 1, 6, 11, what comes next? 🤔

content.subject: maths
content.title: Identifying Lines in Yoga Poses
content.concept: Recognize different types of lines in yoga poses.
content.explanation: In yoga, we can see many lines! Let's circle, tick, and cross the aasanas based on the lines they have! 🧘‍♂️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Tracing Lines
content.concept: Identify and trace different types of lines.
content.explanation: Let's trace the missing lines! Can you find the straight, slanting, and curved lines? 🖊️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Making Shapes with Lines
content.concept: Create new shapes using straight lines.
content.explanation: Let's make new shapes using vertical, horizontal, and slanting lines! What can you create? 🎨

content.subject: maths
content.title: Drawing with Curved Lines
content.explanation: Let's draw clouds and rainbows using curved lines! 🌥️🌈

content.subject: maths
content.title: Creating Designs
content.concept: Make designs using different types of lines.
content.explanation: Use different types of lines to create your own design! How many types can you use? 🖌️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Exploring Creases
content.concept: Discover lines created by folding paper.
content.explanation: When we fold paper, we create creases! Let's see how many we can make together! ✂️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Flowers
content.concept: Learn to count and add flowers for decoration.
content.explanation: Rohan and his friends need flowers for their garlands. Let's count how many flowers they have together! 🌸
examples: Rohan has 10 flowers, Suwali has 2 flowers, together they have 12 flowers.

practice_questions.q: How many flowers are used by Rohan and Suwali together?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Adding Flowers
content.concept: Learn to add two-digit numbers using flowers as an example.
content.explanation: Let's count the flowers! 🌸 If Zoha has 20 flowers and Javed has 43 flowers, how many do they have together? We add them like this: 20 + 43 = 63! 🌼
examples: Zoha has 20 flowers 🌼, Javed has 43 flowers 🌸, together they have 63 flowers 🌺.

practice_questions.q: What is the total number of flowers used by Zoha and Akash?
How many flowers are used by Zoha and Javed in all?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Addition
content.concept: Break down numbers to make addition easier.
content.explanation: We can break down numbers to add them easily! For example, 57 + 34 can be done as 57 + 30 + 4 = 91! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What is 24 + 43?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Using the Number Line
content.concept: Learn to add using a number line.
content.explanation: A number line helps us visualize addition! 🌈 Count the steps on the line to find the answer.
examples: Using a number line: 0 to 10, jump 10 and then 4 to reach 14.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Mahi and Isha's Blocks
content.explanation: Mahi has some blocks and Isha brings her blocks too! Let's find out how many blocks they have together. 🧱
content.narrative: Mahi has 23 blocks and Isha has 38 blocks. Together, they have 61 blocks! Let's add them up: 23 + 38 = 61. 🎉

content.subject: maths
content.title: Solving Real-Life Problems
content.explanation: Let's solve some real-life problems using addition! 🤔
examples: Rahim made 43 runs in one match and 58 runs in another. How many runs did he make in total?

practice_questions.q: Simarpreet had 12 color pencils. Her mother gifted her 36 more. How many does she have now?
Heena collected 34 red marbles and 57 blue marbles. How many total marbles does she have?
Sarika spent ₹56 in the fair, while Manish spent ₹35. How much did they spend together?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Diyas
content.explanation: If you have 75 diyas and sold 35, you can find out how many are left by subtracting. Let's count together! 🍎🍎🍎

practice_questions.q: How many diyas are left if I have 75 and sold 35?
If I have 32 diyas and sold 12, how many are left?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Using the Ginladi
content.concept: Using a number line to visualize subtraction.
content.explanation: We can use a number line (ginladi) to help us subtract! Just count backwards from the number you start with. 🎈

practice_questions.q: What is 26 - 12?
What is 39 - 28?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fill in the Blanks
content.explanation: Let's practice subtraction by filling in the blanks! 📝

content.subject: maths
content.title: Money Subtraction
content.explanation: Let's see how subtraction helps us manage money! 💰 If you have some money and buy something, you subtract the cost.
examples: Shikha has ₹82, bought pencils for ₹22, left with ₹60.
Ruby has ₹60, bought a notebook for ₹20, left with ₹40.

practice_questions.q: If Shikha has ₹82 and spends ₹22, how much does she have left?
If Ruby has ₹60 and spends ₹20, how much does she have left?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Finding Remaining Flowers
content.concept: Subtracting to find out how many flowers are left.
content.explanation: Jyoti has some flowers and gives a few away. Let's find out how many she has left! 🌸

practice_questions.q: Jyoti has 54 flowers and gives 28 to her mother. How many flowers is she left with?
Jyoti has 73 flowers and gives 47 to her mother. How many flowers is she left with?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Let us Do
content.explanation: Let's solve these subtraction problems together! 🧮

content.subject: maths
content.title: Skip the Rope
content.concept: Using subtraction to find out how many more skips are needed.
content.explanation: Aman and Avni are playing a fun game of skipping rope! Let's see how many more skips they need to complete 20! 🤸‍♂️
examples: Aman skipped 14 times. How many more does he need? 20 - 14 = 6 more skips!

practice_questions.q: Aman skipped 14 times. How many more skips does he need to make to complete 20 skips?
Avni skipped 12 times. How many more skips does she need to make to complete 20 skips?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Let us Do
content.explanation: Let's help Anushka and others with their collections! 🐚

practice_questions.q: Anushka collected 63 shells and gave 26 to her brother. How many shells are left?
54 passengers were sitting in the bus and 16 got down. How many are there now?
There were 40 balloons and 13 burst. How many are left?
Kanika made 72 bangles and sold 36. How many are left?
56 birds were sitting on the tree. Now there are 87 birds. How many came later?
Arman dribbled the ball 18 times. How many more to complete 35 taps?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fact Families
content.explanation: Let's fill in the missing numbers to see how addition and subtraction are related! 🔢

content.subject: maths
content.title: A Beautiful Carved Bed
content.explanation: Once upon a time, Raja Jagdeep wanted to make a beautiful carved bed for his queen. Let's see how he planned it! 🛏️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Handspans
content.explanation: A handspan is the distance from the tip of your thumb to the tip of your little finger when your hand is stretched out. Let's see how many handspans different objects are!

practice_questions.q: How many handspans is your desk?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Choosing Paths
content.concept: Finding the longest and shortest paths.
content.explanation: When you need to go somewhere, you can choose different paths. Let's find out which one is the longest and which one is the shortest! 🚶‍♂️
examples: Path A is longer than Path B.
Path B is shorter than Path A.

practice_questions.q: Which path should Kavita take to reach school fast?

content.subject: maths
content.title: How Many Blocks?
content.explanation: Let's stack some blocks to see how tall our toys are! 🧸 How many blocks tall is each toy?

practice_questions.q: How tall is your toy?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Pumpkin's Chaupal
content.explanation: Let's see which vegetables are heavier or lighter! 🥕🍉 Can you guess if a pumpkin is heavier than a watermelon?

practice_questions.q: Which is the heaviest vegetable?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Make Your Own Balance
content.concept: Creating a balance to compare weights.
content.explanation: Let's make a balance using a paper cup and a hanger! ⚖️ You can compare the weights of different objects like an eraser and a ball.

practice_questions.q: What did you find heavier?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Watermelon and Mango
content.concept: Understanding balance through a fun story.
content.explanation: Watermelon wants to play on the see-saw but needs help from his mango friends to balance! 🍉🍑

practice_questions.q: How many mangoes will balance the watermelon?
practice_questions.a: Count the mangoes in the picture!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Finding Your Weight
content.explanation: Let's find out how much you weigh! You can check your weight at home or at the doctor’s office. 🏥
examples: If you weigh 30 kg, how much do your friends weigh?

practice_questions.q: What is your weight?
Name someone who weighs almost the same as you.
practice_questions.a: ___ weighs almost the same as me.

practice_questions.q: Who weighs more than you?
Who weighs less than you?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Ingredients for Nimbu Pani
content.explanation: Raghu made nimbu pani for his grandma! 🍋 Let's help him make it for himself and his friends.
examples: 1 glass of water, 1 spoon of sugar, 2 spoons of lemon juice.

practice_questions.q: How much water do you need for 3 glasses?
How much sugar do you need for 3 glasses?
How much lemon juice do you need for 3 glasses?
How much salt do you need for 3 glasses?

content.subject: maths
content.title: How Much Water Fits?
content.explanation: Raghu's bottle holds 4 glasses of water. How about yours? 🥤 Let's find out!
examples: If your bottle holds 5 glasses, how much more can it hold than Raghu's?

practice_questions.q: How many glasses of water can fill your bottle?
How many glasses can fill your friend's bottle?
practice_questions.a: My friend's bottle can fill ___ glasses.

practice_questions.q: Does your friend's bottle fill with equal, more, or less water?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Making Music with Water
content.explanation: Let's make a jal tarang! Fill glasses with different water levels and tap them to hear sounds. 🎶
examples: Fill one glass halfway and another full to see how the sound changes.

practice_questions.q: What sounds do you hear when you tap the glasses?
How does the sound change with different water levels?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Wheels
content.explanation: Let's count the wheels of cars, bicycles, and autorickshaws! 🚗 How many wheels do we have in total?
examples: 7 cars with 4 wheels each means 7 times 4 equals 28 wheels.

practice_questions.q: How many wheels are there in 2 autorickshaws?
How many wheels are there in 4 bicycles?
How many wheels are there in 7 cars?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Matching Sums
content.explanation: Can you match the sums with their answers? Let's see how many you can get right! 🧩

practice_questions.q: Match 3 + 3 + 3

content.subject: maths
content.title: Adding Numbers
content.concept: Learn how to add numbers together.
content.explanation: Adding numbers means putting them together to find a total! For example, if you have 3 🍎 and you get 3 more 🍎, you have 6 🍎 in total! Let's add: 3 + 3 = 6.

practice_questions.q: What is 5 + 5?
What is 8 + 8?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Multiplication
content.explanation: Multiplication is like adding the same number many times. For example, 3 × 2 means you have 3 🍌 and you want 2 groups of them! So, 3 + 3 = 6 🍌.

practice_questions.q: What is 3 × 4?
What is 5 × 2?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Complete the Multiplication Tables
content.explanation: Let's fill in the multiplication tables! It's fun to see how numbers work together. For example, 2 × 1 = 2, 2 × 2 = 4, and so on!

practice_questions.q: Fill in the blank: 5 × 2 = ____
Fill in the blank: 3 × 3 = ____

content.subject: maths
content.title: Sharing Gulab Jamuns
content.explanation: Sharing means dividing things equally. If you have 8 🍬 and you want to share them with a friend, you can give 4 🍬 to each! Let's share!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Sharing Gulab Jamuns
content.explanation: Once, there were some delicious gulab jamuns 🍬. Everyone wanted to eat them! But they decided to share. They took turns taking 1 for each person. Soon, everyone had some! They learned that sharing is caring! 🎉

content.subject: maths
content.title: Gulab Jamuns Sharing
content.explanation: When we have something to share, we can divide it equally among friends! 🍬 If Ritu has 3 gulab jamuns and you have 3, how many do we have in total? Let's find out! 🤔

practice_questions.q: How many gulab jamuns were there in total?
Have they shared equally? Yes/No
How many gulab jamuns did each of them get?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Ritu’s Art Project
content.explanation: Ritu wants to draw 12 bindis on 2 ice cream cones. How can we help her divide them equally? 🍦🎨

content.subject: maths
content.title: Pooja’s Laddoos
content.explanation: Pooja has 2 plates with laddoos. Let's help her divide them equally into 3 plates! 🍬🍽️
examples: If she has 6 laddoos, each plate will get 2 laddoos!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Garima’s Beads
content.explanation: Garima has 20 star-shaped beads and uses 5 for each bracelet. How many bracelets can she make? 🌟💍

content.subject: maths
content.title: Beads and Strings
content.explanation: If each string has 7 beads, how many strings can we make with 21 beads? Let's find out! 🎉🔗

content.subject: maths
content.title: Tailor and Candles
content.explanation: A tailor has 27 candles and wants to put them equally in 3 boxes. How many candles will be in each box? 🕯️📦

content.subject: maths
content.title: Sharing Bananas
content.explanation: If we have 24 bananas and want to share them equally among 3 monkeys, how many bananas does each monkey get? 🍌🐒

content.subject: maths
content.title: Seasons Poem
content.explanation: Let's enjoy a fun poem about the seasons! 🌞🌧️❄️
content.text:
Summer comes with the shining sun
and a long day for children to play.
Rainy season comes with the water drops;
Peacock dances and the frog hops.
Five seasons in a year
Spring begins with a cheer
Flowers bloom, the birds sing
When comes the merry spring.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Which Season is it?
content.explanation: Let's talk about our favorite seasons and what makes them special! 🌼🍂❄️

practice_questions.q: In which season do you go on a vacation?
Your birthday falls in which season of the year?
Which is your favourite season? Why?
What is special about your favourite season?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Seasons and Festivals
content.explanation: Let's match each season with its festivals! 🎉🌸

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding the Calendar
content.concept: Learning about months and days in a calendar.
content.explanation: A calendar helps us keep track of days, weeks, and months! 🌟 Each month has a different number of days. Let's explore how many days are in each month! 📅

practice_questions.q: Why do we use a calendar?
practice_questions.a: To keep track of days and plan our activities! 🗓️

practice_questions.q: Does February have the same number of days in every year?
practice_questions.a: No, it has 28 days in a common year and 29 days in a leap year! 🎈

practice_questions.q: How many days are there in a week?
practice_questions.a: There are 7 days in a week! 🗓️

practice_questions.q: How many Sundays are there in April?
Which months have 5 Sundays?
practice_questions.a: Months like January and March can have 5 Sundays! 🎊

practice_questions.q: When are summer vacations?
practice_questions.a: In the months of May and June! 🌞

practice_questions.q: When are winter vacations?
practice_questions.a: In the months of December and January! ❄️

practice_questions.q: Find the total number of days in April and March.
practice_questions.a: April has 30 days and March has 31 days, so 30 + 31 = 61 days! 🎉

content.subject: maths
content.title: How Long Does it Take?
content.concept: Matching activities with their time duration.
content.explanation: Different activities take different amounts of time to complete! ⏳ Let's match them up! 🧩
examples: Knitting a sweater takes several days. 🧶

practice_questions.q: How long does cooking take?
practice_questions.a: Cooking usually takes a few hours! 🍽️

practice_questions.q: How long does it take to fill a water tank?
practice_questions.a: It can take several hours depending on the size! 💧

practice_questions.q: How long does a summer vacation last?
practice_questions.a: Summer vacation usually lasts for a few weeks! 🌞

content.subject: maths
content.title: Family Trip Adventure
content.concept: Understanding time and planning during a trip.
content.explanation: Join the family on their exciting trip to Vijayawada! 🚂 Let's see how they manage their time! ⏰
content.narrative: Yes, we will go to Manglagiri hills at 11:00 Morning. Maa! Our train is day after tomorrow. Can we go for sightseeing? Maa! We enjoyed a lot today and look at Mama ji, he is looking very handsome. Yes dear! It’s his wedding after all. God bless you dear. Just 1 more hour to reach Vijayawada Railway Station. Hurry! The train is about to leave. Date: 24 Nov. Time: 8:25 O’clock in the morning. It is 1:15 Afternoon Dada Ji, how much more time will it take to reach Nani’s house? Date: 25 Nov. Time: 10:00 O’clock in the night. Date: 24 Nov. Time: 1:15 O’clock in the afternoon. Date: 26 Nov. Time: 8:00 O’clock in the morning. Children, it’s 5:00 Evening, we will be late. Let us go back. See Dadi, how beautiful these trees and plants are. There are so many beautiful flowers also. We will come back after 8 hours. Ok sir, you visit the fort. I will come after 8 hours to pick you all. Have a nice trip. Oh Papa, our trip ended and now we have to go back to our home. But, I am excited to travel back by the fastest train in India, called Vande Bharat. Mummy, I can’t wait to tell my friends about this exciting trip. Yes, we will make such a trip again very soon! Wow!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Time
content.concept: Learning to read and understand time.
content.explanation: Time helps us know when to do things! 🕒 For example, if you wake up at 7:00 AM, you can plan your day better!

practice_questions.q: What time do you wake up?
practice_questions.a: I wake up at ___ O' clock.

practice_questions.q: When do you go to school?
practice_questions.a: I go to school at ___ O' clock.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Calculating Trip Duration
content.explanation: We can find out how long we spend on trips by subtracting the start time from the end time! ⏳
examples: If we leave at 9:00 AM and return at 5:00 PM, we spent 8 hours!

practice_questions.q: How long was the trip to Manglagiri hills?
How long was the trip to Kondapalli fort?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Sharing Travel Stories
content.explanation: Talking about trips helps us learn about different places and what we enjoy! 🌍
examples: I went to the beach and built sandcastles! 🏖️

practice_questions.q: Have you been on a trip recently?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Direction Poem
content.explanation: This fun poem helps us remember directions! 🧭
content.text:
East, West, North or South
Let us stand to face the rising sun.
This is the...East direction.
To your back you will find the West,
To your left is the North, and to your right is the South!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Gargi's Day Activities
content.explanation: Let's see what Gargi does in a day and at what time! ⏰
examples: Gargi wakes up at 6:00 AM and goes to school at 7:00 AM.

practice_questions.q: At what time does Gargi wake up?
practice_questions.a: She wakes up at ___ O' clock.

practice_questions.q: When does she have lunch?
practice_questions.a: She has lunch at ___ O' clock.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Reading the Clock
content.concept: Learning to read time on a clock.
content.explanation: We can tell time by looking at the hour hand and minute hand on the clock! 🕰️
examples: If the hour hand is on 3 and the minute hand is on 12, it is 3:00.

practice_questions.q: What time is shown on the clock?
Draw the hour hand and minute hand for 5:30.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Directions with a Compass
content.concept: Learn to identify North, South, East, and West using a compass.
content.explanation: A compass is a special tool that helps us find directions. When you hold it, you can see where North is, and from there, you can figure out South, East, and West! 🧭
examples: If you face North, your left side is West and your right side is East.

practice_questions.q: Haneet’s desk is to the ___________ of Sarah’s desk.
Almirah is to the ___________ of Ali’s desk.
To reach the blackboard, Mira must walk towards ___________ and then ___________.
To feed the birds on the window, Nitin has to walk ___________ and then ___________.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Rupal's Adventure at the Fair
content.explanation: Rupal loves going to the fair! 🎡 She had ₹50 from her mom and spent some on rides and snacks. Let's find out how much she spent!
content.narrative: Rupal went to the fair with her friends and had ₹50. She spent ₹20 on rides, ₹20 on snacks, and ₹2 on candies. In the end, she had ₹8 left. How much did she spend? Let's calculate together! 🧮

practice_questions.q: If you had ₹3 left when your father gave you ₹40 for the fair, how much did you spend?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Learning About Indian Currency
content.concept: Identifying different Indian currency notes and coins.
content.explanation: In India, we use different notes and coins for money. Let's learn how to count them! 💰
examples: A ₹500 note has 'Five Hundred' written on it.

practice_questions.q: How many 20 paise will make ₹1?
How many 25 paise will make ₹1?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Matru's Balloon Challenge
content.concept: Calculating money won from a game.
content.explanation: Matru plays a game where he pops balloons to win money! 🎈 Let's see how much he can win!

practice_questions.q: If Matru popped balloons worth ₹3, ₹17, and ₹22, what is the total amount won?
If Matru had four chances, how much could he win?
practice_questions.a: Calculate based on the balloons he pops!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Different Ways to Pay
content.concept: Understanding how to make different amounts of money.
content.explanation: Let's learn how to make the same amount of money using different coins! For example, you can use two ₹20 coins and one ₹10 coin to make ₹50. 💰✨

practice_questions.q: How can you make ₹66?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Buying Items
content.explanation: Let's see how much money you need to buy different items. If you want to buy a toy for ₹30 and you have ₹20, you need ₹10 more! 🛒💵
examples: If you have ₹50 and want to buy a book for ₹45, you have ₹5 left!

practice_questions.q: Jayant spent ₹25 on pens and ₹40 on notebooks. How much did he spend in total?
Ajay has ₹58 and spent ₹48. How much is left?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Favourite Colours
content.explanation: Let's find out which color is the favorite among children! We can fill in a table to see how many kids like each color. 🎨❤️
examples: If 10 kids like red, 5 like blue, and 3 like yellow, we can see red is the favorite!

practice_questions.q: What is the most liked color?
practice_questions.a: The most liked color is red.

practice_questions.q: Which color is liked less than green?
practice_questions.a: Yellow is liked less than green.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Picnic Day
content.explanation: At the fruit party, we can see how many kids like each fruit! 🍎🍌🍊 Let's fill in the table together!
examples: If 5 kids like apples and 3 like bananas, we can compare their favorites!

practice_questions.q: How many children like apples?
practice_questions.a: Fill in the number based on the table.

practice_questions.q: Which fruit is the most liked?
practice_questions.a: The most liked fruit is the one with the highest number.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Number of Students by Transport
content.concept: Understanding how to collect and represent data.
content.explanation: We can find out how many students come to school using different types of transport! 🚶‍♂️🚕🚌🚴‍♂️

practice_questions.q: How many students come using a bike?
practice_questions.a: Fill in the blank with your answer.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Favorite Games
content.explanation: Let's find out which games are the most liked by asking our friends! 🎮🏏

practice_questions.q: What is the most liked game?
practice_questions.a: Fill in the blank with your answer.

practice_questions.q: Which game is liked more than football?
practice_questions.a: Fill in the blank with your answer.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Visiting Families
content.concept: Collecting data about families and grandparents.
content.explanation: Let's visit families and find out how many have grandparents living with them! 👵👴

practice_questions.q: How many families did you visit?
practice_questions.a: Fill in the blank with your answer.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Family Members
content.concept: Understanding the number of family members.
content.explanation: Ask your friends how many people live in their homes and fill in the table! 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦

practice_questions.q: How many families have more than 4 people?
practice_questions.a: Fill in the blank with your answer.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Connecting Shapes
content.explanation: Draw lines to connect similar shapes without crossing them! 🎨

practice_questions.q: Can you connect all the shapes?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Math Number Trick
content.concept: Using numbers to create fun tricks.
content.explanation: Think of a number, double it, add eight, and see if you can get 4! 🤔
examples: Start with 2, double is 4, add 8 is 12, minus 2 is 10, not 4!

practice_questions.q: Can you make it work with your number?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Number Names from 11 to 20
content.explanation: Can you find the number names hidden in the letters? 🔍

practice_questions.q: What number names can you find?

content.subject: maths
content.title: What is my shape?
content.explanation: I am in a bicycle, And in a car and a bus, I make all vehicles run, They can’t move if I am flat. What is my shape? 🤔

content.subject: maths
content.title: Complete the equations
content.explanation: Let's practice some math! Can you fill in the blanks using addition or subtraction? 🧮

practice_questions.q: What is 18 - 9?
What is 6 + 3?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Joining Dots
content.explanation: How many rectangles can you make by joining the dots? Remember, a square is also a rectangle! 📏

content.subject: maths
content.title: Shepherd's Garden
content.explanation: A shepherd has 4 sheep and 4 dogs. Can you divide the garden into 4 areas, so each area has one tree, one sheep, and one dog? 🐑🐶🌳

content.subject: maths
content.title: Arrange the Numbers
content.concept: Arranging numbers to achieve equal sums.
content.explanation: How will you arrange the numbers 1 to 7 so that the three arms have the same total? 🤔🔢

content.subject: maths
content.title: Think of a Number
content.explanation: I think of a number and double it. If my answer is 18, what was the number? Can you find it? 🔍

content.subject: maths
content.title: Pressing Keys
content.concept: Using a calculator to achieve a specific number.
content.explanation: Press 10 keys on a calculator to make 28. Can you figure out how? 🖱️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding the Foundational Stage
content.concept: The importance of early years in education.
content.explanation: The first eight years of a child's life are super important! 🌟 They help shape how we learn, behave, and grow. Just like planting a seed, if we take care of it early, it will grow strong! 🌱
examples: A child learns to share toys with friends 🤝
A child learns to count apples 🍎🍏

practice_questions.q: Why are the first eight years important?
practice_questions.a: They shape our learning and behavior!

content.subject: maths
content.title: 5+3+3+4 Curricular Structure
content.concept: The structure of education in India.
content.explanation: In India, we have a special way to learn called the 5+3+3+4 structure! 📚 It helps us learn step by step, starting from ages 3 to 8! 🎓

practice_questions.q: What does 5+3+3+4 mean?
practice_questions.a: It's how we learn in different stages!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Learning Through Play
content.concept: The importance of play in learning.
content.explanation: Learning can be fun! 🎉 When we play, we learn new things without even realizing it! Like counting blocks while building a tower! 🏗️
examples: Playing with toys to learn shapes 🔺🔵

practice_questions.q: How can we learn while playing?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Count the Fruits
content.explanation: Let's count some fruits together! 🍎🍌🍊 How many fruits do you see? Count them out loud! 📢

practice_questions.q: How many apples are there?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Numbers
content.concept: Numbers help us count and measure things.
content.explanation: Numbers are like magic symbols that help us count things! For example, if you have 🍎🍎🍎, you have 3 apples! Let's learn how to recognize and write numbers.

practice_questions.q: How many stars are there? ⭐⭐⭐

content.subject: maths
content.title: Adding Numbers
content.explanation: When we add, we combine two groups! If you have 🍎🍎 and I give you 🍎, how many do you have now? Let's count together! 🍎🍎 + 🍎 = 🍎🍎🍎.

practice_questions.q: What is 1 + 2?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Subtracting Numbers
content.explanation: When we subtract, we take away some items! If you have 🍎🍎🍎 and you eat 1, how many are left? 🍎🍎🍎 - 🍎 = 🍎🍎.

practice_questions.q: What is 3 - 1?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fun with Numbers
content.explanation: Let's solve these fun problems together! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What is 4 + 2?
What is 5 - 3?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Joyful Learning in Mathematics
content.concept: Learning math through play and activities.
content.explanation: Mathematics can be fun! We learn by playing games and doing activities that help us understand numbers and shapes. 🎲✨
examples: Using toys to learn shapes like circles and squares 🔵🔲

content.subject: maths
content.title: Developing Mathematical Ideas
content.explanation: We use our imagination and play to understand how numbers work and how to think about space! 🧠🔍

content.subject: maths
content.title: Discussing Math Concepts
content.concept: Talking about math through stories and poems.
content.explanation: We can talk about math while enjoying stories and poems! It makes learning exciting! 📖🎶
examples: Discussing the poem 'Seasons' and how they relate to numbers 🌸🌞🍂❄️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Seasons
content.concept: Learning about seasons through a poem.
content.explanation: This poem helps us understand the seasons and how they change! 🌼☀️🍁❄️
content.text:
In spring, flowers bloom, 🌸
In summer, sun shines bright, ☀️
In autumn, leaves fall down, 🍂
In winter, snow is white. ❄️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Building Skills through Activities
content.concept: Practicing math skills through fun activities.
content.explanation: We practice our math skills by doing fun activities that help us learn! 🎉✏️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Integrating Learning
content.explanation: Math is everywhere! We learn it along with languages and physical activities! 🌍📚🤸‍♂️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding through Picture Stories
content.concept: Using stories to introduce math concepts.
content.explanation: We can learn math through fun stories! 📖 For example, 'Heena' and 'Aatif' help us understand numbers and shapes.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Math in Our Daily Lives
content.explanation: Math is everywhere! 🌍 We can count diyas during festivals or share fruits in the park. Let's explore!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Learning Through Activities
content.explanation: Activities like origami and stamping help us learn math skills while having fun! 🎨✂️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Playing with Numbers
content.explanation: Games make learning exciting! 🎲 Let's play 'Let us Make 100' and 'Flash Card Game' to practice our math skills.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fun with Puzzles
content.concept: Solving puzzles to enhance critical thinking.
content.explanation: Puzzles are a fun way to challenge our minds! 🧩 Let's solve some together and see how many ways we can find answers.
examples: What comes next in the pattern? 🔢

content.subject: maths
content.title: Exploring Learning Opportunities
content.concept: Learning happens everywhere, not just in books.
content.explanation: Children can learn by observing their surroundings, talking to family, and engaging in various activities like playing and traveling. 🌍👨‍👩‍👧‍👦
examples: Watching a nature documentary on TV 🦁
Building a toy model with friends 🧸

content.subject: maths
content.title: What Did You See?
content.explanation: Look around your home or school and write down three things you see. 🏠👀

practice_questions.q: What did you see outside today?
practice_questions.a: I saw a bird, a tree, and a car.

content.subject: maths
content.title: The Wisdom of Grandparents
content.explanation: Listening to stories from grandparents can teach us important lessons about life and values. 📖👵👴

content.subject: maths
content.title: Learning is Fun!
content.concept: Learning can happen in many ways.
content.explanation: This rhyme helps us remember that learning is all around us! 🎶
content.text:
Learning is fun, oh yes it is! 🎉
In the park or in the fizz! 🌳
With friends and family, we explore,
Every moment opens a door! 🚪

content.subject: maths
content.title: A Day at the Beach
content.concept: Learning to count objects in groups.
content.explanation: Let's count the seashells we find at the beach! 🐚 If we have 3 groups of 2 seashells each, how many do we have in total? That's right, 6 seashells! 🎉

practice_questions.q: If you have 4 groups of 2 starfish, how many starfish do you have?
Count the number of crabs if there are 5 groups of 3 crabs each.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Shapes Around Us
content.explanation: Look around! Can you see a ball? ⚽ That's a sphere! Let's find more shapes like cubes and cylinders in our room! 🏠

practice_questions.q: What shape is a basketball?
Name a shape that has 6 square faces.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fun with Numbers
content.explanation: Numbers are like magic! ✨ Let's count from 1 to 100 together! Can you say 'one hundred'? That's the biggest number we will learn today! 🎈

practice_questions.q: What comes after 25?
Count how many fingers you have!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Shadow Story (Togalu)
content.explanation: Once upon a time, there were two friends, a triangle and a square. They loved to play hide and seek in the park! 🌳 Can you guess which shapes they made with their shadows? 🌞
content.narrative: The triangle made a pointy shadow, while the square made a flat shadow. They laughed and played until the sun went down! 🌅

practice_questions.q: What shape is the shadow of a pizza?
Can you find a shape that has four equal sides?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Playing with Lines
content.explanation: Let's play with lines! 📏 A straight line can go up, down, left, or right. Can you draw a line that goes across your paper? 🎨

practice_questions.q: Draw a line that goes up!
What do you call a line that goes side to side?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Decoration for Festival
content.concept: Learning addition and subtraction through decoration.
content.explanation: It's festival time! 🎉 Let's decorate with balloons! If we have 5 balloons 🎈 and we add 3 more 🎈, how many do we have? That's right, 8 balloons! 🎈🎈🎈🎈🎈🎈🎈🎈

practice_questions.q: If you have 10 candies and eat 2, how many are left?
Add 4 apples to 3 apples. How many apples do you have?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Rani’s Gift
content.explanation: Rani received a gift that was 10 cm long! 🎁 Can you measure how long your pencil is? Let's see if it's longer or shorter! ✏️

practice_questions.q: If a book is 15 cm long, how many cm longer is it than a 10 cm pencil?
Measure your desk. Is it more than 50 cm?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Grouping and Sharing
content.concept: Learning multiplication and division through grouping.
content.explanation: Let's share cookies! 🍪 If we have 12 cookies and we want to share them equally among 4 friends, how many does each friend get? That's right, 3 cookies each! 🎉

practice_questions.q: If you have 20 candies and share them with 5 friends, how many does each friend get?
Multiply 3 groups of 4 apples. How many apples do you have?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Which Season is it?
content.concept: Understanding the measurement of time through seasons.
content.explanation: What season is it? 🌼 Let's learn about spring, summer, autumn, and winter! Each season lasts about 3 months. Can you guess which season it is now? 🌞

practice_questions.q: How many months are in a season?
What comes after summer?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fun at the Fair
content.concept: Learning about money through fair activities.
content.explanation: Let's go to the fair! 🎡 If a ticket costs 5 coins and you have 20 coins, how many tickets can you buy? That's right, 4 tickets! 🎟️

practice_questions.q: If you have 15 coins and a toy costs 3 coins, how many toys can you buy?
Count how many coins you have if you have 10 coins and find 5 more!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Data Handling
content.explanation: Let's collect data! 📊 If we count how many pets our friends have, we can make a chart! How many have dogs, cats, or birds? Let's find out! 🐶🐱🐦

practice_questions.q: If 4 friends have 2 dogs each, how many dogs are there in total?
Count how many friends have cats. If 3 friends have cats, how many cats are there?
